channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is defending President Donald Trump 's firing of the nation's senior military officer and a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon, insisting that they weren't unusual despite accusations that the new administration is injecting politics into the military
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A look at Dan 'Razin' Caine, Trump's pick to be the top US military officer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s choice to be the top U.S. military officer, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, endeared himself to the commander in chief through his military call sign, “Razin,” during a meeting in Iraq years ago.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nonprofit working on Mississippi River project loses federal funding
item.description: Mississippi Park Connection said funds for restoration work that has not yet been completed are frozen, as are funds for congressionally funded work that has already been completed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: NIH funding freeze stalls applications on $1.5 billion in medical research funds
item.description: The National Institutes of Health had to stop considering new grant applications, delaying funding for research into diseases ranging from heart disease and cancer to Alzheimer’s and allergies.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following an altercation with a counter protester.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Fired employees fear beloved Yosemite National Park will lose its luster
item.description: Yosemite National Park employees who recently were fired by the U.S. government worry cuts will affect the experience of visitors and the welfare of wildlife that thrive in the popular vacation destination located in California’s Sierra Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Haven Daley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michigan state lawmaker beats out loyal Trump ally charged in fake elector case for GOP party chair
item.description: Michigan Republicans have elected a state lawmaker to lead the GOP into the 2026 midterms, State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House and Ukraine nearing rare earths deal that would tighten relationship, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House and Ukraine have made significant progress toward reaching an agreement that would provide the U.S. with access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals and tighten the long-term relationship between Kyiv and Washington, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump’s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday largely blocked sweeping executive orders from President Donald Trump that seek to end government support for programs promoting diversity, equity and inclusion. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump's second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon says it will cut 5,400 probationary workers starting next week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Defense Department said Friday that it’s cutting 5,400 probationary workers starting next week and will put a hiring freeze in place.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top immigration enforcement official reassigned amid frustrations over mass deportation effort
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The top official in charge of carrying out President Donald Trump's mass deportations agenda has been reassigned amid concerns that the deportation effort isn't moving fast enough.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: ‘In Trump We Trust’ Is Exploding
item.description: President Trump forced through his Cabinet – we told you that would only increase his control over the Republican Party, and it did. To that point, Matt Lewis headlines in The Hill, “Conservatism used to mean something. Now it’s just whatever Trump says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Black farmers among those left in limbo amid federal funding freeze
item.description: The federal government has historically discriminated against Black farmers. The recent funding halt from the Trump administration presents yet another hurdle to those who have spent decades fighting for equity in farming.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza, MPR News

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emotional testimony for 'Jaya's Law' argues for tougher penalties for wrong-way drivers
item.description: A Las Vegas family made an emotional plea to state lawmakers on Tuesday, telling the story of 3-year-old Jaya Brooks, who was killed in a crash involving a wrong-way driver in December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida cabinet assumes new role, sparking criticism over privacy and power
item.description: After a month-long feud over who would be the state's chief immigration enforcement officer, state lawmakers passed a bill package that grants not one, but four elected officials with that task.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada may expand revenge porn law to cover AI
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend the state’s revenge porn law to include computer-generated images.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Senator Brian Jones to propose bill that would reform sanctuary state practices
item.description: Senator Brian Jones is looking to propose a bill that would shake up current legislation that shapes California as a sanctuary state.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada lawmakers target 'resort fee'-style rent practices, age for gun ownership
item.description: Hidden fees are so out-of-hand that it now costs money just to pay the rent. And it's a good bet that tenants aren't told that until they have signed the lease.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5
